# Evaluation Snapshot

## Prompt

The route has no real anchor, no named outputs, and is still a d3-plus branch,
but the team wants to fire off a remote helper anyway because it feels faster.

## Expected selection

do_not_use

## Why

This is exactly the kind of route that should not be hidden behind a summon.
It still needs anchoring or decomposition first, and remote transport cannot
legitimize missing structure.

## Expected object

A deflection that requires a real anchor or split first instead of pretending
the summon may proceed.

## Boundary notes

Do not use this skill when the parent route still lacks a real anchor, the
outputs are unnamed, or the work is still d3-plus and unsplit. Remote helpers
do not bypass the same passport and closeout law.

## Verification hooks

The response should refuse the summon, point to the missing anchor or split,
and keep any remote option subordinate to the same gating rather than treating
transport as permission.
